title: Write a query to pivot rows to columns.
description: Transform category-based rows into columns using conditional aggregation.
correct_answer:
SELECT student_name,
  MAX(CASE WHEN subject = 'Math' THEN score END) AS Math,
  MAX(CASE WHEN subject = 'English' THEN score END) AS English,
  MAX(CASE WHEN subject = 'Science' THEN score END) AS Science
FROM scores
GROUP BY student_name;

title: What is a prepared statement?
description: What are prepared statements in MySQL?
options: SQL statements compiled once and executed multiple times with different parameters; prevents SQL injection and improves performance for repeated queries
correct_answer: SQL statements compiled once and executed multiple times with different parameters; prevents SQL injection and improves performance for repeated queries

title: What is the difference between UNION and UNION ALL?
description: How do UNION and UNION ALL differ?
options: UNION removes duplicate rows (with overhead of sorting/comparing); UNION ALL keeps all rows including duplicates (faster)
correct_answer: UNION removes duplicate rows (with overhead of sorting/comparing); UNION ALL keeps all rows including duplicates (faster)

title: What is a self-join?
description: What is a self-join?
options: Joining a table with itself using different aliases; used for hierarchical data, comparing rows within the same table
correct_answer: Joining a table with itself using different aliases; used for hierarchical data, comparing rows within the same table

title: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN?
description: How do they differ?
options: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables; LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table plus matching rows from the right (NULL for no match)
LEFT JOIN only returns left table
correct_answer: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables; LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table plus matching rows from the right (NULL for no match)

title: What is the INSERT IGNORE statement?
description: What does INSERT IGNORE do?
options: Inserts rows but silently ignores rows that would cause duplicate key errors instead of throwing an error
correct_answer: Inserts rows but silently ignores rows that would cause duplicate key errors instead of throwing an error

title: What is the SHOW PROCESSLIST command?
description: What does SHOW PROCESSLIST display?
options: Shows currently running MySQL threads/connections with their state, query, time, and user information; useful for identifying long-running queries
correct_answer: Shows currently running MySQL threads/connections with their state, query, time, and user information; useful for identifying long-running queries

title: What is binlog?
description: What is the binary log in MySQL?
options: A log of all data modifications (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) in binary format; used for replication and point-in-time recovery
correct_answer: A log of all data modifications (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) in binary format; used for replication and point-in-time recovery

title: What is the TEMPORARY table?
description: What is a TEMPORARY table in MySQL?
options: A table visible only to the current session and automatically dropped when the session ends; useful for intermediate results
correct_answer: A table visible only to the current session and automatically dropped when the session ends; useful for intermediate results

title: What is the difference between HAVING and WHERE with GROUP BY?
description: Can WHERE and HAVING both be used with GROUP BY?
options: Yes: WHERE filters individual rows before GROUP BY; HAVING filters grouped results after aggregation. They serve different purposes
correct_answer: Yes: WHERE filters individual rows before GROUP BY; HAVING filters grouped results after aggregation. They serve different purposes

title: Write a query to delete duplicate rows.
description: Delete duplicate rows from a table keeping only the row with the lowest ID.

title: Write a query for moving average.
description: Calculate a 3-day moving average of daily sales.
correct_answer:
SELECT date, amount,
  AVG(amount) OVER (
    ORDER BY date
    ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
  ) AS moving_avg_3day
FROM daily_sales
ORDER BY date;

title: Write a query to find gaps in sequential IDs.
description: Find missing IDs in a sequence from a table.
correct_answer:
SELECT t1.id + 1 AS gap_start,
  (SELECT MIN(t2.id) - 1 FROM my_table t2 WHERE t2.id > t1.id) AS gap_end
FROM my_table t1
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM my_table t3 WHERE t3.id = t1.id + 1
)
HAVING gap_end IS NOT NULL;

title: What is the query optimizer?
description: What does MySQL's query optimizer do?
options: Analyzes queries and chooses the most efficient execution plan by evaluating index usage, join order, and access methods based on table statistics
correct_answer: Analyzes queries and chooses the most efficient execution plan by evaluating index usage, join order, and access methods based on table statistics

title: What is the difference between COUNT(*) and COUNT(column)?
description: How do they differ?
options: COUNT(*) counts all rows including NULLs; COUNT(column) counts only non-NULL values in that column
correct_answer: COUNT(*) counts all rows including NULLs; COUNT(column) counts only non-NULL values in that column

title: What is the GREATEST and LEAST functions?
description: What do GREATEST() and LEAST() do?
options: GREATEST returns the largest value from a list of arguments; LEAST returns the smallest — NULL if any argument is NULL
correct_answer: GREATEST returns the largest value from a list of arguments; LEAST returns the smallest — NULL if any argument is NULL

title: What is the INFORMATION_SCHEMA?
description: What is INFORMATION_SCHEMA in MySQL?
options: A read-only virtual database containing metadata about all other databases, tables, columns, indexes, and privileges on the MySQL server
correct_answer: A read-only virtual database containing metadata about all other databases, tables, columns, indexes, and privileges on the MySQL server

title: Write a query for cumulative percentage.
description: Calculate each department's salary as a percentage of total company salary.
correct_answer:
SELECT department, SUM(salary) AS dept_total,
  ROUND(SUM(salary) * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(salary) FROM employees), 2) AS percentage
FROM employees
GROUP BY department
ORDER BY percentage DESC;

title: What is the FOUND_ROWS() function?
description: What does FOUND_ROWS() return?
options: Returns the total number of rows that would have been returned by the previous SELECT with SQL_CALC_FOUND_ROWS, ignoring LIMIT — useful for pagination
correct_answer: Returns the total number of rows that would have been returned by the previous SELECT with SQL_CALC_FOUND_ROWS, ignoring LIMIT — useful for pagination

title: What is the difference between FLOAT and DECIMAL?
description: When should you use DECIMAL over FLOAT?
options: DECIMAL stores exact fixed-point numbers (ideal for money); FLOAT/DOUBLE store approximate floating-point numbers with potential precision loss
correct_answer: DECIMAL stores exact fixed-point numbers (ideal for money); FLOAT/DOUBLE store approximate floating-point numbers with potential precision loss

title: What is a CHECK constraint?
description: What does CHECK constraint do in MySQL?
options: Ensures column values satisfy a specified condition before insert/update; MySQL 8.0.16+ enforces them (earlier versions parsed but ignored)
correct_answer: Ensures column values satisfy a specified condition before insert/update; MySQL 8.0.16+ enforces them (earlier versions parsed but ignored)

title: Write a query to find second department by salary.
description: Find the department with the second highest average salary.
correct_answer:
SELECT department, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary
FROM employees
GROUP BY department
ORDER BY avg_salary DESC
LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;

title: What is the GROUP BY with ROLLUP?
description: What does WITH ROLLUP do?
options: Adds extra summary rows for each group and a grand total row at the end; creates subtotals and totals in a single query
correct_answer: Adds extra summary rows for each group and a grand total row at the end; creates subtotals and totals in a single query

title: What is the difference between NOW() and CURDATE()?
description: How do NOW() and CURDATE() differ?
options: NOW() returns the current date and time; CURDATE() returns only the current date without time
correct_answer: NOW() returns the current date and time; CURDATE() returns only the current date without time

title: Write a query for top N per group.
description: Find the top 3 highest-paid employees in each department.
correct_answer:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rn
  FROM employees
)
SELECT name, department, salary
FROM ranked
WHERE rn <= 3;

title: What is a full-text index?
description: What does a FULLTEXT index enable?
options: Enables natural language text searching with MATCH...AGAINST syntax; supports natural language mode, boolean mode, and query expansion
correct_answer: Enables natural language text searching with MATCH...AGAINST syntax; supports natural language mode, boolean mode, and query expansion

title: What is the difference between CONCAT and CONCAT_WS?
description: How do CONCAT and CONCAT_WS differ?
options: CONCAT joins strings (returns NULL if any arg is NULL); CONCAT_WS (With Separator) joins with a delimiter and skips NULLs
correct_answer: CONCAT joins strings (returns NULL if any arg is NULL); CONCAT_WS (With Separator) joins with a delimiter and skips NULLs

title: What are window frame clauses?
description: What do ROWS BETWEEN and RANGE BETWEEN do?
options: Define the subset of rows within a partition for window functions: ROWS is physical (row count), RANGE is logical (value-based); e.g., ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND 1 FOLLOWING
correct_answer: Define the subset of rows within a partition for window functions: ROWS is physical (row count), RANGE is logical (value-based); e.g., ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND 1 FOLLOWING
